hero.title: TAINOS CYBER CON
hero.tagline: Cybersecurity in the age of AI, cloud, and persistent threats

about.title: ABOUT
about.subtitle: Where cybersecurity meets the public
about.description: Tainos Cyber Con is an international cybersecurity awareness conference offering training, talks, and workshops. Our mission: provide equitable access to security solutions across all communities.
about.location_detail: 3235 Ave de la Gare, Mascouche, Room 2C2B

benefits.title: Threat Clarity
benefits.desc: Understand current risks and anticipate tomorrow's threats.

benefits.title: Practitioner Sessions
benefits.desc: Real cases and deployable tools presented by security professionals.

benefits.title: Timely Topics
benefits.desc: Content carefully selected based on industry trends.

benefits.title: Networking
benefits.desc: Connect with 50+ Quebec cybersecurity professionals and decision-makers.

items.title: When AI Becomes Cybercriminals' Weapon of Choice
items.desc: Deepfakes, automated phishing, adaptive attacks: understand the mechanics of these threats to better decide where to invest your defenses.

items.title: Defensive AI: Turning Cybersecurity into an Intelligent Immune System
items.desc: From the theory of behavioral detection to its concrete deployment within your infrastructure.

items.title: Regulation, Ethics and Trust: Building Responsible AI
items.desc: Translating compliance (Bill 25, GDPR, AI Act) into concrete technical requirements and reputational advantages.

items.title: Cybersecurity in the Quantum Era
items.desc: Why your current encryption will become obsolete, and how to start a post-quantum migration now.

items.title: The Risks of AI: Between Hype and Blind Spots
items.desc: Hallucinations, algorithmic bias, excessive dependency and loss of control: understanding the real dangers behind the promise of AI, and the safeguards to put in place before an incident occurs.

items.title: OT Security: Protecting the Connected Factory in the Age of Automation and AI
items.desc: The convergence of IT and OT worlds now exposes industrial systems, PLCs and production lines to unprecedented cyber threats. Understand how automation and AI both strengthen resilience and expand the attack surface, and adopt a protection strategy suited to critical environments.

items.title: Incident Response: From Detection to Recovery
items.desc: When a cyberattack strikes, every minute counts. Building an effective incident response capability: clear action plans, well-trained teams, forensic tools and crisis communication. How AI and automation accelerate detection and remediation, and why prior preparation determines success.

speakers.title: OUR SPEAKERS
speakers.subtitle: Announced progressively starting June 2026
speakers.cta_desc: Practitioners, researchers, and decision-makers — share your expertise with our community.
speakers.call_intro: We are launching the call for speakers for this edition of Tainos Cyber Con 2026. If you have knowledge, field experience, or research that can help our audience better understand, protect, and anticipate cybersecurity threats, your voice belongs on this stage.

confirmed.name: Mike Arbrouet
confirmed.title: Field CTO, IBM
confirmed.bio: Technology leader guiding organizations through the complexity of AI, cloud, and constant change. Builder of secure, scalable solutions and featured speaker at AFROTECH™ 2025.

confirmed.name: Blaise Arbouet
confirmed.title: Co-founder, YottaSec Inc.
confirmed.bio: Cyber Evangelist and information security governance expert. CISSP, C-CISO, ISO 27001. Co-founder of Tainos CyberCon and YottaSec Inc., focused on digital inclusion for developing nations.

confirmed.name: Valentin Bromont
confirmed.title: Incident Response Practice Lead, National Bank of Canada
confirmed.bio: Incident response and cyber crisis management expert. Leads the incident response practice at the National Bank of Canada, guiding organizations through complex cyber threats.

confirmed.name: Anglade Perrier
confirmed.title: Founder & vCISO, TEKAP
confirmed.bio: Cybersecurity and risk governance expert, Anglade Perrier is the founder of TEKAP, a Quebec-based firm specializing in vCISO services, compliance (CMMC, SOC2, ISO 27001), and risk management. Holding the ASC, C.Dir, and NACD.DC designations, he helps organizations protect their digital assets and strengthen their security posture.

confirmed.name: Jean-François Brouillette
confirmed.title: Manager - Surveillance, Incident Response and Digital Forensics
confirmed.bio: Professional with over a decade of experience, starting in IT systems management before specializing in cybersecurity. He has worked as a crisis management consultant during cyberattacks for major organizations worldwide, and currently supports the National Bank of Canada's cyber defense team. His mission: prevent cyberattacks and complicate the lives of cybercriminals.

confirmed.name: Dr. Abdoul Karim Ganame
confirmed.title: Founder & Chief of Cybersecurity, StreamScan
confirmed.bio: Ph.D. in Cybersecurity with 19 years of IT experience, including 16 years specializing in information security. Founder of StreamScan, he pioneered an AI-powered threat detection system (CDS) recognized by the Canadian federal government as an innovation. Expert in IDS, MDR/SOC services, CMMC compliance, and cyber incident response, serving Energy, Aerospace & Defense, Manufacturing, and Public Sector organizations across Canada and internationally.

confirmed.name: Christian Kengne
confirmed.title: Principal Director of Cybersecurity Technologies, Fiera Capital
confirmed.bio: ing., ASC, C.Dir., CISSP. Computer Engineering graduate with a Master's in AI (Université de Sherbrooke), Christian Kengne is a recognized cybersecurity leader named among Canada's Top 50 Most Influential Cybersecurity Experts on LinkedIn (2024) and IFSEC Global Top Influencer (2021). Previously Director of Cyber-Resilience Consulting at Accenture, he now leads cybersecurity strategy at Fiera Capital and sits on the boards of the Quebec Revenue Agency and Mobility Infra Quebec. Active member of ISACA, OWASP, and the Quebec Order of Engineers.

confirmed.name: Stéphane Gagnon
confirmed.title: Cybersecurity Expert & Instructor, Collège de Maisonneuve
confirmed.bio: Cybersecurity, information security, and IT governance expert with over 12 years of teaching experience at Collège de Maisonneuve, where he trains the next generation of IT and cybersecurity professionals. A former Assistant Director responsible for information security and IT infrastructure at Cégep de Saint-Jérôme, he also served as Chief Information Security Officer (CISO), leading initiatives in risk management, service continuity, incident response, information asset protection, and cybersecurity governance framework implementation.

confirmed.name: Evan Wagner
confirmed.title: Founder, HackNWA
confirmed.bio: Cyber humanitarian and founder of HackNWA, Evan Wagner works at the intersection of technical cybersecurity and community engagement. Holding GCIA and MSFPCS certifications, he specializes in command and control (C2) infrastructure and implant tooling, while organizing events and delivering training to empower communities through education, collaboration, and hands-on practice.

confirmed.name: Nourhene Ben Youssef
confirmed.title: Full Professor & Program Coordinator, IT Governance, Audit & Security — Université de Sherbrooke
confirmed.bio: Ph.D. in Accounting (UQAM), Nourhene Ben Youssef is a Full Professor at the École de gestion of Université de Sherbrooke and coordinator of the graduate programs in IT governance, audit, and security. Her research focuses on financial information disclosure, corporate governance, accounting irregularities, and financial restatements. At the intersection of accounting and cybersecurity, she trains the next generation of IT governance professionals.

confirmed.name: Alice A-Khalil
confirmed.title: Member of the National Assembly of Québec (Fabre) & Parliamentary Assistant to the Minister of Cybersecurity and Digital Affairs
confirmed.bio: Elected to the National Assembly of Québec and recognized expert in information technology and cybersecurity. With over 30 years of experience in the field, she has led strategic projects in architecture, application integration, security, monitoring, and threat management. Multilingual (French, English, Arabic, and Serbian), her international expertise allows her to navigate the intersection of technological, political, and societal issues. Her career has taken her to collaborate with major industry players, negotiate with vendors, and support the digital transformation of large organizations.

why.title: Why attend this conference?
why.subtitle: One day. Four concrete benefits for you and your organization.

benefits.text: A clear read on 2026 threats: ransomware, offensive AI, cloud compromise — understand what's actually targeting you.
Multiple practitioner-led sessions with real-world cases and tools you can deploy the very next day.
Carefully curated topics that are current and directly relevant to your industry.
50+ Quebec cybersecurity professionals and decision-makers in one room — the ideal moment to build relationships that matter.

sponsors.title: PARTNERS
sponsors.subtitle: Become a Tainos Cyber Con 2026 partner
benefits: Targeted visibility to 200+ attendees over three months

mission.title: Cyber Con Corporation
mission.subtitle: Democratizing cybersecurity across the Caribbean and North America
mission.statement:
We believe cybersecurity is not a privilege reserved for the few. Since 2018, Cyber Con Corporation has organized summer conferences in Haiti, placed security certification vouchers in the hands of students who could never afford them, and provided hands-on training to underserved communities. We have seen firsthand how closing the digital divide transforms lives.

As we scale our impact across the Caribbean and North America, we relaunch our flagship program under the name Tainos Cyber Con — named after the Taíno people, the indigenous inhabitants of the Caribbean, whose resilience and interconnectedness inspire our mission. The conference is one program among many. Our work continues year-round: scholarships, training partnerships, and community programs designed to bring cybersecurity to those who need it most.
mission.description: Cyber Con Corporation is a Florida-based 501(c)(3) nonprofit founded in 2018 in Pensacola, Florida. Our mission: provide cybersecurity education, training, and resources to low-income and underserved communities across the Caribbean and North America.

pillars.title: Conferences & Training
pillars.desc: Summer conferences organized in Haiti to educate and train local communities in cybersecurity.

pillars.title: Certification Vouchers
pillars.desc: Security certification vouchers distributed to students who would otherwise have no means to obtain them.

pillars.title: Training Access
pillars.desc: Hands-on training programs that close the digital divide for underserved communities.

pillars.title: Caribbean Expansion
pillars.desc: Under the Tainos Cyber Con banner, we are extending our mission across the Caribbean and North America.

mission.taino_text: The Taíno people were the original inhabitants of the Greater Antilles — Haiti, Cuba, Puerto Rico, Jamaica. Their resilience, their sense of community, and their interconnectedness across the islands embody exactly what we are building: a cybersecurity network rooted in Caribbean identity, reaching across borders to lift every community.

programs.name: Tainos Cyber Con
programs.desc: The annual international cybersecurity conference — our flagship program, now open to the Caribbean and North America.

programs.name: Certification Vouchers
programs.desc: Funding for recognized certifications (CompTIA, CISSP, CEH) for eligible low-income students.

programs.name: Community Training
programs.desc: Workshops and awareness sessions held in communities throughout the year.

mission.donate_text: Your donation funds certification vouchers, training programs, and conferences in communities that need them most. Cyber Con Corporation is a 501(c)(3) organization — donations are tax-deductible in the United States.
mission.page_intro: Tainos Cyber Con is the flagship program of Cyber Con Corporation — a 501(c)(3) organization working since 2018 to democratize access to cybersecurity in underserved communities across the Caribbean and North America.
who: Students without access to cybersecurity certifications
Low-income communities across the Caribbean and North America
Underrepresented professionals in the technology sector
Young people seeking a career in cybersecurity
